ShortDescription.Value: Ships phantom-vacancy and office AI fixes plus experimental software import corrections.

Publish.LongDescription:
No Office Demand Fix currently ships confirmed office-demand fixes plus a narrower software troubleshooting path in Cities: Skylines II.
- Signature Phantom Vacancy fix: removes stale PropertyOnMarket and PropertyToBeOnMarket state from occupied Signature office and industrial properties so false free-space counts stop suppressing demand. - Office AI chunk-iteration hotfix: one low-stock office no longer stops later offices in the same chunk from consuming stock and queuing virtual exports. - Office-demand baseline direct patch: restores the older `2x` office resource-demand baseline instead of the newer vanilla `3x` multiplier so evidence remains comparable to earlier runs. - Software import seller correction: a narrow fallback that lets office virtual-resource imports consider outside connections when vanilla seller selection is too restrictive. - Software import buyer timing correction: a narrow fallback buyer for zero-weight office inputs that remain below threshold without a buyer, path, trip, or current trading state. - Diagnostics: optional logs for office demand, phantom vacancy, and software office state; disabled by default until you turn them on for troubleshooting.
Current release status:
- fixes the reproduced Signature phantom vacancy case - fixes the vanilla office AI chunk-iteration abort on low stock - restores the pre-hotfix office-demand baseline for comparability via a direct Harmony patch - includes experimental software import seller and buyer corrections by default, with diagnostics available but disabled by default - keeps broader software-related office stalls under investigation - keeps non-signature phantom vacancy under observation
Publish.ChangeLog: - Added the office AI chunk-iteration hotfix so one low-stock office no longer aborts later offices in the same chunk. - Restored the older `2x` office-demand baseline with a direct Harmony patch for comparability against earlier evidence. - Kept the experimental software import seller and buyer corrections enabled by default while making diagnostics opt-in until you need bounded evidence capture. - Kept the broader software path positioned as investigational rather than a claimed full fix.
